member.name: Mr Clive Betts

addresses: Clive Betts MP, Office 1B, G2, Leeds Road, Sheffield, S9 3TY

member.synopsis: Mr Clive Betts is the Labour MP for Sheffield South East , and has been an MP continually since 9 April 1992.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Slaughterhouses: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will direct the FSA to publish the terms of reference and timetable for conduct of their review of current and emerging abattoir technologies and their potential regulatory use including what
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) works closely with the meat industry on the introduction of new technology in abattoirs. Whilst it is for businesses to determine what technology they wish to invest in and deploy, the FSA is keen to collaborate on these in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Slaughterhouses: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when the FSA last reviewed and updated the scientific evidence, research and data underpinning the Official Controls legislation referred to in PQ Answer 120762.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2022, the Food Standards Agency (FSA) completed an operational transformation programme and launched a new operational modernisation programme in 2023. These aim to improve the quality and cost effectiveness of the delivery of official controls through...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food Poisoning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many major food incidents have there been in each of the last 5 years and of those how many were meat related.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In responding to food and feed safety incidents and foodborne disease outbreaks, the Food Standards Agency (FSA) categorises its handling of incident responses at Routine or Non-routine levels. Non-routine incident and outbreak responses are escalated whe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Inspections

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) does not centrally record billing errors for Official Controls in the format requested. Therefore, it is not possible to provide a definitive count of billing errors made in charging food business operators for Official Con...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Marwan Barghouti
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether she has made representations to representatives of the Government of Israel on the treatment of Marwan Barghouti.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is deeply concerned by reports of mistreatment of detainees by Israeli forces and raises this issue with the Israeli government. The UK has been consistently clear that all detainees must be treated with dignity and in full accordance with internat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Transplant Surgery
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans his Department has to consider transplants, including stem cell and bone marrow transplants, as part of the Getting It Right First Time programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no current plans to consider transplants, including stem cell and bone marrow transplants, as part of the Getting It Right First Time programme. The Department is aware of challenges around the timely provision of well-matched stem cell donors, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Stem Cell Strategic Forum’s 2022 report, A ten-year vision for stem cell transplantation and cellular therapies, set out a number of recommendations for improving data collection for haematopoietic stem cell transplantation and cellular therapy (HS...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) has for many years published comprehensive information on how its meat charges are made up annually. This information can be found in Cost Data Slides available on its website, at the following link:https://www.food.gov.uk/...

writtenQuestions.heading: Slaughterhouses: Regulation

writtenQuestions.answerText: The official controls legislation, Regulation (EU) 2017/625, sets clear requirements to undertake ante-mortem and post-mortem official inspections and the verification of compliance with food law, animal health, and welfare rules in slaughterhouses and ga...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will bring food businesses responsible for processing meat and meat products in line with the regulatory approach used elsewhere in the food industry by allowing them to be fully responsible
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) supports evidence‑based use of technology in Official Controls, but inspection requirements in abattoirs are set in assimilated European Union law Regulation, and are prescriptive, and divergence could restrict British acce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans he has to introduce more effective uses of technology and science evidence-based approaches to the delivery of Official Controls by the Food Standards Agency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) supports evidence‑based use of technology in Official Controls, but inspection requirements in abattoirs are set in assimilated European Union law Regulation, and are prescriptive, and divergence could restrict British acce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food Standards Agency: Parliamentary Scrutiny
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that the Food Standards Agency is subject to an appropriate level of Parliamentary scrutiny.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Food Standards Agency (FSA) was established by the Food Standards Act 1999 as a non-ministerial Government department. The FSA is accountable to the Westminster Parliament, the Senedd, and the Northern Ireland Assembly through health ministers in Engl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Blood Cancer: Immunotherapy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that access to CAR-T therapies for mantle cell lymphoma is consistent across the UK, in the context of its continued availability in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) is re-evaluating brexucabtagene autoleucel following managed access through the Cancer Drugs Fund to determine whether it should be recommended for routine National Health Service funding. NICE’...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hotels: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the business rates system on the hotel sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Budget, the VOA announced updated property values from the 2026 revaluation. This revaluation is the first since the pandemic, which has led to significant increases in rateable values for some properties as they recover from the pandemic. To respo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Hotels: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she has taken to ensure that the hotel sector has not been disproportionately impacted by the rise in business rates.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Hotels: Business Rates

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important contribution that the hotel and wider hospitality sectors make to the economy, to local communities and to the UK’s appeal as a destination for domestic and international tourists. The potential impacts of changes o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will consider the capital funding needs of adult social care as part of the next Spending Review. We have recently announced an additional £50 million for the Disabled Facilities Grant (DFG) in 2025/26. This could fund approximately 5,000 h...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: We are progressing towards a National Care Service based on greater choice and control, joined up services, and higher quality of care, with over £4.6 billion of additional funding available for adult social care by 2028/29 compared to 2025/26. We have no...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
